steps.title: Notice the same line
steps.content:
The second equation is the first equation multiplied by $4$.
$$4(7x+6y)=4(5)$$
$$28x+24y=20$$
So any point on $7x+6y=5$ lies on both graphs.

steps.title: Check the parameterized point
steps.content:
Use $x=-\frac{6r}{7}+\frac{5}{7}$ and $y=r$ in the simpler equation.
$$7\bigl(-\frac{6r}{7}+\frac{5}{7}\bigr)+6r=-6r+5+6r$$
$$-6r+5+6r=5$$

steps.title: Calculator shortcut
steps.content: Define $L(x,y)=7x+6y$ and test the parameterized points. The matching point form keeps $L(x,y)=5$ for every value of $r$. The answer is D.
